> Thank you for taking part in this experiment!
> Before we walk you through the tasks you will be doing, we would like to get a sense of what
> content you typically consume on the Reddit platform. On the preceeding page, we ask that you
> input the names of the Subreddits you typically engage with in any capacity. (i.e. writing posts,
> reading posts, upvoting, commenting, sharing, etc.) On the page following that, we ask that you input
> your general interests, independent of specific Subreddits. To input a response, type it within the
> provided box and press ENTER after every response. Keep in mind, there will be a three minute
> time limit. However, if you feel you are done before time runs out, press continue. This data
> is crucial to the experiment, so please be as accurate possible when typing your responses.
> After you complete these tasks, you will be provided further instructions and continue to the actual experiment.
> When you are ready to start, press Continue.

> You will now proceed with the acutal experiment.
> This experiment has two tasks:
> <u>In the first task</u>, you will be presented the name of a category and must type
> in as many items that correspond to that category as you can within the time limit.
> In the second task, you will move each of the words that you typed in the first
> task around so that the similar ones are proportionately closer together.
> The experiment will consist of only 3 ""trials,"" so please be as accurate/precise as
> possible when typing in items and placing the objects, rather than trying to speed through.
> Before you get started, we just want to get you familiar with the experiment layout. We'll walk you through
> a practice verbal fluency and spatial arrangement task and provide more detailed instructions.
> When you are ready to start, press Continue.

>You are about to perform a verbal fluency task.
> In this task, you will be presented the name of a category (e.g., FURNITURE) in capital
> letters. Your job is to type in as many items as you can think of that correspond to that
> category in 3 minutes. You will be doing this three times on three separate domains.

> Now, you will begin a spatial arrangement task.
> In this task, you will be shown the set of words you typed in the last verbal fluency task you performed.
> We need to know how similar you think these words are to one another. Your job is to move each word
> around so that the similar words are proportionately closer together. That is, you should move them into
> space such that the distance between each pair of items represents how similar you think the pair is.
> (with closer in space meaning more similar, and farther away in space meaning more dissimilar)

column1: <video width = ""600"" height = ""400"" autoplay muted loop style = ""margin-top: 10px

column2: > The words will appear one by one in the top left corner of a box. You can move the words by simply
> click/dragging them. If the words seem similar, place them close together. If they seem dissimilar,
> place them farther away from each other. You will be doing this three times on three separate domains,
> with a different set of words each time, based the words you typed in the corresponding verbal fluency
> task. After dragging each word, the next word will appear. You may rearrange previously placed items.
> Note, to complete the trial, all items must be moved into the ""active arena,"" the large central box.
> When you are finished arranging the space, click on the little Continue button at the bottom of the screen.
> Let's continue with our practice round!
> When you're ready, press Begin.
